cot_prefix: Okay, I'm given a question asking which of the options is a humorous edit of the artist or movie name "mean girls." These types of edits are often used for humor, sometimes in social media, memes, or internet culture. (B) oean girls - This changes the first letter "m" to an "o," making it "oean." The word "oean" doesn't really mean anything, and it doesn't seem to be a homophone or a word that would be humorous. Again, "meyan" isn't a real word and doesn't seem to have a humorous meaning. This appears to be a random misspelling without a comedic twist. This is a common type of humorous edit, where a word is spelled the way it sounds. People often use this kind of spelling to create a humorous or silly effect.
